method_atom: Analysis of a Hamiltonian dynamical system whose trajectories consist of a smoothly integrable flow interrupted by specular reflections at domain boundaries, reduced to a discrete return map ('bouncing map') whose Jacobian yields the linear stability and bifurcation thresholds of periodic orbits. When the return map has a twist structure it admits a generating function enabling perturbative classification of orbits; the framework establishes persistence of invariant tori for sufficiently smooth boundaries, construction of adiabatic invariants valid over exponentially long times in a strong-perturbation limit, and, in other regimes, a transition from regular motion to ergodic/chaotic behavior.
object_atom: A charged billiard ball moving in a plane connected (possibly unbounded) domain while immersed in a homogeneous stationary magnetic field perpendicular to the plane — including billiards in a square, in convex smooth-boundary domains, and scattering off two circles.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the return-map / twist-map analysis of a piecewise-smooth Hamiltonian system (stability via Jacobian, KAM tori, adiabatic invariants, onset of ergodicity), while the magnetically-immersed charged ball bouncing in a planar domain is the physics-specific object.
